name: M04 Garganta del Oso Trail
name_original: Espacio Natural de la Sierra de Candelario – variante a la Garganta del Oso (M04)
description: This moderate 14.3-mile mountain biking trail in Sierra de Candelario features a waterfall, pine forests, and views of Candelario.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Béjar are set within the Sierra de Béjar, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, featuring green leafy forests and steep mountains with altitudes up to 2,425 meters. The region's diverse ecosystems include oak, chestnut, ash, and strawberry-tree forests, with rivers like the Cuerpo de Hombre flowing from the mountain summits. This landscape provides an extensive network of trails, including ancient paths and mountain passes, ideal for varied mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Béjar The most popular mountain bike trail is Dehesa de Candelario Trail, a 18.0 miles (29.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route navigates through the region's characteristic forests. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is M04 Garganta del Oso Trail, a moderate 14.3 miles (23.0 km) path. This route offers a journey through varied terrain, including sections along the Cuerpo de Hombre river. Local mountain bikers also love the Cancho La Muela and Navamuño Reservoir Loop, a 16.1 miles (26.0 km) trail leading through diverse environments, often completed in about 2 hours 32 minutes. Mountain biking around Béjar is defined by coniferous and hardwood forests, ancient paths, and challenging mountain passes. The extensive network of routes offers options for all ability levels, from light options to more technical enduro trails. The routes in Béjar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Béjar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Béjar region?
A: The Béjar region offers a wide array of mountain biking opportunities, with over 50 routes available on komoot. These trails traverse the diverse landscapes of the Sierra de Béjar, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Béjar?
A: Yes, Béjar has options for less experienced riders and families. While many routes are moderate or difficult, there is at least one easy trail available. The region's extensive network, including the Red Ciclista Sierra de Béjar, offers routes categorized by difficulty, so you can find suitable options for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Béjar?
A: Mountain bike trails in Béjar are characterized by diverse terrain within the Sierra de Béjar. You'll encounter green leafy forests, steep mountains, and varied ecosystems including oak, chestnut, ash, and strawberry-tree forests. Many routes follow ancient paths and integrate challenging mountain passes, with some sections running alongside rivers like the Cuerpo de Hombre.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in the Béjar area?
A: Yes, the Béjar region offers longer routes for those seeking an extended ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993810525/bejar-montemayor-del-rio-variante-circular-a-la-ruta-de-los-tartesos-por-la-via-verde-de-la-plata-m15" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Béjar - Montemayor del Río – variante circular a la ruta de Los Tartesos por la Vía Verde de la Plata (M15)&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 38.3 km route that incorporates the historic Vía de la Plata.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Béjar?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Béjar are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993794793/cancho-la-muela-and-navamuno-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho La Muela and Navamuño Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 26.0 km through diverse environments.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or viewpoints accessible from the mountain bike trails?
A: The Béjar region is rich in natural beauty. You can find stunning viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2712929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ambroz Valley and Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5432000" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navamuño Reservoir Views&lt;/a&gt;. The Sierra de Béjar also features significant peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2671693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreón (Calvitero Summit)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/904739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Negra Summit&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites to explore near the mountain bike routes?
A: Beyond the trails, Béjar offers several cultural and historical attractions. The town itself features a magnificent medieval wall, Romanesque churches, and the Ducal Palace. You can also visit "El Bosque," a Renaissance garden with historical design, ponds, and fountains, or explore the route of the textile factories to learn about the town's industrial past.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Béjar?
A: The diverse landscapes of Béjar make it suitable for mountain biking across different seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. While the Sierra de Béjar-La Covatilla ski resort provides winter sports opportunities, some higher-altitude trails might be affected by snow in winter. Always check local conditions before heading out.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the mountain bike trails in Béjar?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many trails in natural areas like the Sierra de Béjar, provided they are kept under control. However, it's always advisable to check specific regulations for individual routes or protected areas to ensure dog-friendly access and to be mindful of local wildlife and livestock.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Béjar?
A: The mountain bike trails in Béjar are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the beauty of the coniferous and hardwood forests, and the challenging nature of many routes, which offer a rewarding experience for all skill levels.

Q: Is public transport available to access mountain bike trails in Béjar?
A: While the region's trails often follow ancient paths connecting villages, specific public transport links directly to trailheads are not widely detailed. Access is generally easier by car, allowing you to reach various starting points. However, some routes, like those along the Vía de la Plata, might be accessible from towns with public transport connections.

Q: Where can I find parking when mountain biking in Béjar?
A: Parking is typically available in or near the towns and villages that serve as starting points for the trails. For routes that begin deeper in natural areas, look for designated parking spots or roadside pull-offs near trail access points. It's always a good idea to plan your route and check for parking options beforehand.
tips: This wonderful waterfall known as Garganta del Oso is located a few kilometers from the picturesque town of Candelario and is undoubtedly one of the many attractions offered by the Sierra de Béjar.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the M04 Garganta del Oso Trail?
faqs.answer: The M04 Garganta del Oso Trail is categorized as moderate for mountain biking, suggesting varied conditions. Riders should expect some unpaved or challenging segments, possibly corresponding to an STS S2 difficulty level. It requires good physical condition and cycling experience.

faqs.question: What natural features and viewpoints can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter incredible panoramas of the charming village of Candelario and the imposing Béjar mountain range. The trail also passes through young Scots pine forests and areas with broom shrubs. A key highlight is the Garganta del Oso itself, a beautiful waterfall where water erosion has sculpted unique rock formations and small pools.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to ride the M04 Garganta del Oso Trail?
faqs.answer: The M04 Garganta del Oso Trail is located within the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de Candelario. While specific permits for mountain biking are not mentioned, it's always advisable to check local regulations for natural parks regarding access and activities before your visit. Generally, there are no entrance fees for the natural space itself.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the M04 Garganta del Oso Trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking locations for the M04 Garganta del Oso Trail are not detailed, but given its proximity to Candelario, parking options would likely be available in or near the village. It's recommended to research parking facilities in Candelario before your trip.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, moderate mountain biking trails in the Sierra de Candelario region are generally best enjoyed during spring and autumn. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and stable weather conditions, avoiding the intense heat of summer or potential snow and ice in winter at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Is the M04 Garganta del Oso Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As the trail is within a natural space, dogs are generally allowed but typically must be kept on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always practice Leave No Trace principles and clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: The M04 Garganta del Oso Trail is rated as moderate and requires good physical condition and cycling experience. It is not recommended for beginners or young children who are new to mountain biking. More experienced riders will find it a rewarding challenge.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to eat near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near the charming village of Candelario, which offers various amenities. You can expect to find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options in Candelario, making it a convenient base for your mountain biking adventure.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the M04 Garganta del Oso Trail?
faqs.answer: The M04 Garganta del Oso Trail is approximately 23 kilometers long with an elevation gain of about 780 meters. On average, it takes around 2 hours and 23 minutes to complete by mountain bike, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Does the M04 Garganta del Oso Trail offer a loop route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the M04 Garganta del Oso Trail is designed as a loop, allowing you to start and finish at the same point without needing to retrace your steps. This provides a continuous and varied mountain biking experience.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's moderate difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate cycling gear, and sun protection. A helmet is essential, and a first-aid kit is always recommended. Consider bringing a camera for the scenic views.
